title: Midnight Chinese Noodles Zha Jiang Mian
description: A resinous and velvety Chinese dish featuring fermented soybean paste, served with noodles and vegetables, perfect for midnight cravings. This Buddhist-temple-friendly version uses casabe and papillote cooking methods.
instructions: Cook the noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
In a small bowl, mix the fermented soybean paste with 10ml of vegetable oil until well combined.
Add the crushed casabe to the bowl and stir until the mixture forms a smooth paste.
Cut the papillote paper into 4 equal squares and place a portion of the noodles in the center of each square.
Spoon the soybean paste mixture over the noodles, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Top the noodles with sliced scallions, cucumber, carrots, grated ginger, and minced garlic.
Fold the papillote paper over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Heat 10ml of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat and cook the papillote packets for 2-3 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
Serve the Midnight Chinese Noodles Zha Jiang Mian hot, garnished with additional scallions and soy sauce if desired.
